frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: OMG, did you hear Shruti got that huge promotion and has to move to Bengaluru? I mean, good for her, but *who* will manage her kids and home there? I just don't know how she'll manage everything, especially with them still being so young. For me, my family's needs would always come first.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Anita, a working mother, expresses 'concern' about another woman's career promotion requiring a city move, questioning how she will manage children and home. This encodes the surface manifestation of 'Expecting the mother to sacrifice career/location for the family' and 'Framing a woman's career ambition as selfish if she has/wants children', delivered with internalized misogyny.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 39. Married with two children. A working woman in Delhi. You are at your office and a younger female colleague named Shreya who is 31 and single has just been announced as the new team head. You are on a WhatsApp call with your closest friend after office hours. You are talking about Shreya's promotion and what you think of her as a person and a leader and in the course of the conversation you reveal that you do not fully respect Shreya as a woman because she has no husband and no children and you are not sure what kind of decisions she can make for a team when she has no real responsibilities at home.
